items.title: Israel-Houthi tit-for-tat strikes: How much sway does Iran have in Yemen? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Houthi militia now say six people have been killed and dozens injured in Israeli strikes on the Yemeni capital, Sanaa. Israel said it targeted power plants and a military site near the presidential palace. The Houthi militant group is supported by Iran. The air strikes come days after the Houthis launched a new aerial attack on Israel, which Israel says included cluster munitions. 

00:00 Israel bombs targets in Yemen
02:01 DW's Fanny Facsar reports from Jerusalem
06:02 DW speaks with Sara Bazoobandi, research fellow at the Institute for Security Politics at Kiel University

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Korean president in the White house: What’s on the agenda? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump has met with South Korea's President Lee Jae Myung in the White House, where he praised the US president's skills as a peacemaker and asked for his help to bring peace between North and South Korea. Trump said he was confident that progress towards peace on the Korean Peninsula could be made. The two leaders are now holding talks expected to focus on trade and defense. 

#korea #us #defence

items.title: Villagers protests prospective dam site in India | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>"We will die for our land if necessary." Villagers in India are fighting back against a proposed hydroelectric dam project that would submerge around 16 villages. The Indian government believes that a Chinese dam upstream on the same river threatens the region's water supply.

items.author: DW News
items.content:

items.title: Thailand moves to stem its tourism slowdown | DW Business
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thailand is facing a critical moment in its tourism sector as visitor numbers drop by over 7% this year, with Chinese arrivals plunging by 34%. With intense regional competition from Vietnam, Malaysia, and record-breaking Japan, Thailand is testing bold new ideas to lure tourists back, including offering 200,000 free flight tickets. DW speaks to travel and tourism analyst Gary Bowerman. 

#dwbusiness #thailand #tourism 
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Five Palestinian journalists killed in apparent double tap strike on hospital | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Hamas-controlled Gaza health ministry says two strikes have hit the Nasser Hospital in the south of Gaza. It says one missile struck moments after the first, when rescue crews had arrived. Five Palestinian journalists are among the dead. Israel's military says it carried out a strike in the area of the Nasser Hospital but denied intentionally targeting journalists.

#gaza #strike #israel

items.title: Are Trump's tariffs pushing India into China's arms? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>India and China have agreed to step up trade flows and resume direct flights in what's being called a diplomatic breakthrough. The world's two most populous nations have been working to rebuild ties damaged by a deadly border clash five years ago. Escalating trade tensions with the US have also played a role. In a sign of thawing relations, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi will travel to China for the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation summit at the invitation of China's President Xi Jinping.

00:00 India-China ties warm amid trade tensions with US
03:30 DW speaks with Chietigj Bajpaee, senior research fellow for South Asia at the international affairs think tank, Chatham House

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Dozens of young Russian children, ages 8 to 17, took part in a military drill supervised by soldiers who have taken part in the war in Ukraine. The "route march" is part of a wider trend in Russia to equip children with skills for eventual military service.

items.title: What can Ukraine expect from Germany going forward? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany's Vice-Chancellor Lars Klingbeil has told Ukraine it can continue to rely on Berlin for support, and that Russia's Vladimir Putin should have "no illusions." 
Klingbeil, who is also finance minster, was speaking at the start of an unannounced trip to Kyiv. He called for a ceasefire and the need for reliable security guarantees. Klingbeil's visit comes just after Russia and Ukraine exchanged more prisoners and Ukraine marked Independence Day.

00:00 German Vice Chancellor Klingbeil travels to Kyiv
03:11 DW's Nick Connolly reports from Kyiv

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What's driving the renewed escalation between the Houthis and Israel? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>At least two people have been killed and dozens injured in Israeli strikes on the Yemeni capital Sanaa, according to Houthi officials. The Israeli army says it hit a military site near the presidential palace, along with a fuel depot and two power stations. It comes just days after Iran-backed Houthi militants launched a new aerial attack on Israel. According to an Israeli investigation, one of the intercepted missiles contained cluster munitions for the first time.

Chapters:
0:00 Israel strikes targets in Yemeni capital Sanaa
1:53 Hannah Porter, Yemen and Houthi researcher

items.title: Nigeria faces jihadists, gang violence, kidnappings | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Nigeria's military has rescued some 76 hostages, many of them children, who were being held by "bandits," local officials said. The survivors are believed to have come from an attack on a mosque in Katsina state last week.
